This document describes the error response format, all error codes, rejection scenarios, performance characteristics, and examples.\n6\t\n7\t## Quick Reference: All Error Codes\n8\t\n9\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Category | Description |\n10\t|------------|-------------|----------|-------------|\n11\t| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Authentication | Authorization header or X-Amz-Credential query parameter is missing |\n12\t| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | Authentication | The provided access key does not exist in credentials store |\n13\t| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | 403 | Authentication | Calculated signature does not match the provided signature |\n14\t| `InvalidAlgorithm` | 403 | Authentication | Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported |\n15\t| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Authentication | Credential format is invalid (insufficient parts) |\n16\t| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | Authentication | Authorization header is missing required fields |\n17\t| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Authentication | Request timestamp is outside allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n18\t| `MissingDateHeader` | 403 | Authentication | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n19\t| `InvalidDateFormat` | 403 | Authentication | X-Amz-Date header format is invalid (not ISO 8601) |\n20\t| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Authorization | ACL restrictions prevent access to requested bucket/key |\n21\t| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | Request | HTTP method is not supported for the requested endpoint |\n22\t| `InternalError` | 500 | Server | Server encountered an error during request processing |\n23\t\n24\t## Error Response Format\n25\t\n26\t## Error Response Format\n27\t\n28\tAll error responses follow the S3 XML error format with consistent headers:\n29\t\n30\t### HTTP Headers\n31\t\n32\t```http\n33\tContent-Type: application/xml\n34\tStatus: 403 Forbidden (for authentication errors)\n35\t```\n36\t\n37\t### Response Body\n38\t\n39\t```xml\n40\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n41\t<Error>\n42\t  <Code>ErrorCode</Code>\n43\t  <Message>Human-readable error description</Message>\n44\t</Error>\n45\t```\n46\t\n47\t## Authentication Error Codes\n48\t\n49\t### Invalid Credentials\n50\t\n51\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n52\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n53\t| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records. | Access key not found in credentials store |\n54\t| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | 403 | The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. | Secret key is incorrect or signature is invalid |\n55\t| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing authentication token. | Authorization header is completely missing |\n56\t| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format. | Credential string has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n57\t\n58\t### Malformed Authorization Headers\n59\t\n60\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n61\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n62\t| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | The authorization header is malformed. | Required components missing (Credential, SignedHeaders, or Signature) |\n63\t| `InvalidAlgorithm` | 403 | The authorization header algorithm is not supported. | Algorithm is not AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 |\n64\t| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Access Denied. | Generic authentication failure |\n65\t\n66\t### Timing Issues\n67\t\n68\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n69\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n70\t| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired. | Request timestamp is outside the allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n71\t| `MissingDateHeader` | 403 | Missing required header: x-amz-date. | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n72\t| `InvalidDateFormat` | 403 | Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header. | X-Amz-Date header is not in ISO 8601 format (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ) |\n73\t\n74\t## Authorization and Access Control Errors\n75\t\n76\t### ACL-Based Access Denial\n77\t\n78\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n79\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n80\t| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Access Denied. | Credential exists but ACL restrictions prevent access to the requested bucket/key |\n81\t\n82\t**ACL Access Control Scenarios:**\n83\t\n84\tARMOR supports bucket and prefix-based access control lists (ACLs). When a credential has ACL restrictions configured, the following scenarios will result in `AccessDenied` errors:\n85\t\n86\t1. **Bucket Mismatch** - Credential's ACL bucket restriction doesn't match the requested bucket\n87\t2. **Prefix Mismatch** - Credential's ACL prefix restriction doesn't match the requested key\n88\t3. **No Matching ACL** - No ACL entry allows access to the requested bucket/key combination\n89\t\n90\t**Example:**\n91\t\n92\t```yaml\n93\t# Credential configuration\n94\tcredentials:\n95\t  RESTRICTEDKEY:\n96\t    access_key: RESTRICTEDKEY\n97\t    secret_key: SECRET123...\n98\t    acls:\n99\t      - bucket: \"data-bucket\"\n100\t        prefix: \"allowed/\"\n101\t```\n102\t\n103\tWith this configuration:\n104\t- ✓ `GET /data-bucket/allowed/file.txt` - Success\n105\t- ✗ `GET /data-bucket/forbidden/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n106\t- ✗ `GET /other-bucket/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n107\t\n108\t## Malformed Signature Scenarios\n109\t\n110\tARMOR validates signature format and provides specific error codes:\n111\t\n112\t| Scenario | Error Code | Performance |\n113\t|----------|------------|-------------|\n114\t| Non-hex signature characters | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n115\t| Too short signature (< 32 bytes) | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n116\t| Empty signature | `IncompleteSignature` | < 50ms |\n117\t| Random characters in signature | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n118\t\n119\t## Query Parameter Authentication Errors\n120\t\n121\tARMOR supports authentication via presigned URLs using query parameters. These scenarios can result in authentication errors:\n122\t\n123\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n124\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n125\t| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing Authentication Token | X-Amz-Credential query parameter is missing |\n126\t| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format | X-Amz-Credential has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n127\t| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist | Access key from credential parameter not found |\n128\t| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | Authorization header is missing required fields | X-Amz-SignedHeaders or X-Amz-Signature query parameter is missing |\n129\t| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired | Presigned URL has exceeded its expiration time (X-Amz-Expires) |\n130\t\n131\t**Presigned URL Format:**\n132\t```\n133\thttps://bucket.s3.amazonaws.com/key?\n134\t  X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&\n135\t  X-Amz-Credential=ACCESSKEY/DATE/REGION/s3/aws4_request&\n136\t  X-Amz-Date=YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ&\n137\t  X-Amz-Expires=SECONDS&\n138\t  X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&\n139\t  X-Amz-Signature=CALCULATED_SIGNATURE\n140\t```\n141\t\n142\t## Request Method Errors\n143\t\n144\tARMOR validates that the HTTP method is appropriate for the requested operation:\n145\t\n146\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n147\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n148\t| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | Method {METHOD} not allowed | HTTP method is not supported for the requested endpoint |\n149\t\n150\t**Examples:**\n151\t- Unsupported POST operation on endpoints that only support GET\n152\t- Using DELETE on read-only operations\n153\t\n154\t## Internal Server Errors\n155\t\n156\tThese errors indicate server-side problems during request processing:\n157\t\n158\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n159\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n160\t| `InternalError` | 500 | Failed to {operation}: {error} | Server encountered an error during request processing |\n161\t\n162\t**Common InternalError Scenarios:**\n163\t- Encryption key derivation failures\n164\t- DEK generation failures\n165\t- IV generation failures\n166\t- Header encoding failures\n167\t- Upload failures\n168\t- Temporary file creation failures\n169\t\n170\t## Performance Guarantees\n171\t\n172\tAll authentication rejection scenarios respond within strict time limits:\n173\t\n174\t| Test Type | Target | Actual | Environment |\n175\t|-----------|--------|--------|-------------|\n176\t| Unit test rejections | < 100ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n177\t| Integration test rejections | < 500ms | < 50ms | Real server |\n178\t| Malformed signature rejections | < 50ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n179\t\n180\t### Performance Test Coverage\n181\t\n182\tThe test suite includes performance verification for all rejection scenarios:\n183\t\n184\t1. **TestInvalidCredentialRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 100ms response time\n185\t2. **TestMalformedSignatureRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 50ms response time for malformed signatures\n186\t\n187\t## Error Message Quality\n188\t\n189\tAll error responses include:\n190\t\n191\t1. **Specific Error Code** - Identifies the exact problem (e.g., `InvalidAccessKeyId`, `SignatureDoesNotMatch`)\n192\t2. **Meaningful Message** - Human-readable description of the problem\n193\t3. **XML Format** - S3-compatible XML structure with proper escaping\n194\t\n195\t### Message Validation\n196\t\n197\tTest suite verifies that:\n198\t- Error messages are never empty\n199\t- Messages are at least 10 characters long\n200\t- Messages contain relevant keywords (authentication, signature, credential, algorithm, header, aws4)\n201\t- XML is properly escaped to prevent injection\n202\t\n203\t## Response Consistency\n204\t\n205\tAll error responses maintain consistency:\n206\t\n207\t### Headers\n208\t- Always return `Content-Type: application/xml`\n209\t- Always return appropriate HTTP status code (403 for auth errors)\n210\t- Response body is never empty\n211\t\n212\t### Structure\n213\t- XML declaration with encoding: `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`\n214\t- Root element: `<Error>`\n215\t- Two child elements: `<Code>` and `<Message>`\n216\t- Proper XML escaping for special characters\n217\t\n218\t## Test Coverage Summary\n219\t\n220\tThe ARMOR test suite includes comprehensive coverage for rejection scenarios:\n221\t\n222\t### Unit Tests (`invalid_credential_test.go`)\n223\t- 12 test scenarios covering:\n224\t /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n272\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n273\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=INVALIDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=abc123...\n274\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n275\t```\n276\t\n277\t**Response:**\n278\t```http\n279\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n280\tContent-Type: records.</Message>\n286\t</Error>\n287\t```\n288\t\n289\t### Example 2: Missing Authentication\n290\t\n291\t**Request:**\n292\t```http\n293\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n294\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n295\t```\n296\t\n297\t**Response:**\n298\t```http\n299\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n300\tContent-Type: application/xml\n301\t\n302\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n303\t<Error>\n304\t  <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n305\t  <Message>Missing authentication token.</Message>\n306\t</Error>\n307\t```\n308\t\n309\t### Example 3: Expired Request\n310\t\n311\t**Request:**\n312\t```http\n313\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n314\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n315\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=!@#$%^&*()\n316\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n317\t```\n318\t\n319\t**Response:**\n320\t```http\n321\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n322\tContent-Type: application/xml\n323\t\n324\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n325\t<Error>\n326\t  <Code>SignatureDoesNotMatch</Code>\n327\t  <Message>The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided.</Message>\n328\t</Error>\n329\t```\n330\t\n331\t### Example 4: ACL Access Denied\n332\t\n333\t**Request:**\n334\t```http\n335\tGET /protected-bucket/admin/config.yaml HTTP/1.1\n336\tHost: protected-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n337\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=RESTRICTEDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=valid123...\n338\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n339\t```\n340\t\n341\t**Configuration:**\n342\t```yaml\n343\tcredentials:\n344\t  RESTRICTEDKEY:\n345\t    access_key: RESTRICTEDKEY\n346\t    secret_key: SECRET123...\n347\t    acls:\n348\t      - bucket: \"protected-bucket\"\n349\t        prefix: \"public/\"\n350\t```\n351\t\n352\t**Response:**\n353\t```http\n354\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n355\tContent-Type: application/xml\n356\t\n357\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n358\t<Error>\n359\t  <Code>AccessDenied</Code>\n360\t  <Message>Access Denied</Message>\n361\t</Error>\n362\t```\n363\t\n364\t### Example 5: Expired Presigned URL\n365\t\n366\t**Request:**\n367\t```http\n368\tGET /data-bucket/file.csv?\n369\t  X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&\n370\t  X-Amz-Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250701/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request&\n371\t  X-Amz-Date=20250701T120000Z&\n372\t  X-Amz-Expires=3600&\n373\t  X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&\n374\t  X-Amz-Signature=abc123...\n375\tHTTP/1.1\n376\tHost: data-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n377\t```\n378\t\n379\t**Response:**\n380\t```http\n381\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n382\tContent-Type: application/xml\n383\t\n384\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n385\t<Error>\n386\t  <Code>RequestExpired</Code>\n387\t  <Message>Request has expired</Message>\n388\t</Error>\n389\t```\n390\t\n391\t### Example 6: Invalid Date Format\n392\t\n393\t**Request:**\n394\t```http\n395\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n396\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n397\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=valid123...\n398\tX-Amz-Date: July-14-2025\n399\t```\n400\t\n401\t**Response:**\n402\t```http\n403\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n404\tContent-Type: application/xml\n405\t\n406\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n407\t<Error>\n408\t  <Code>InvalidDateFormat</Code>\n409\t  <Message>Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header</Message>\n410\t</Error>\n411\t```\n412\t\n413\t## Implementation\n414\t\n415\tError responses are generated by two implementations:\n416\t\n417\t1. **Server Handler** (`internal/server/server.go:796-805`)\n418\t   ```go\n419\t   func (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n420\t       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n421\t       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n422\t       // XML generation with proper escaping\n423\t   }\n424\t   ```\n425\t\n426\t2. **Handlers Package** (`internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:2695-2704`)\n427\t   ```go\n428\t   func (h *Handlers) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n429\t       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n430\t       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n431\t       // XML generation with proper escaping\n432\t   }\n433\t   ```\n434\t\n435\tBoth implementations ensure:\n436\t- Consistent Content-Type header\n437\t- Proper XML escaping to prevent injection\n438\t- S3-compatible format\n439\t\n440\t## Testing\n441\t\n442\tTo run the error response test suite:\n443\t\n444\t```bash\n445\t# Run all rejection tests\n446\tgo test -v -run ARMOR Error Response Documentation\n\n## Overview\n\nARMOR provides S3-compatible error responses for all authentication, authorization, and request processing failures. This document describes the error response format, all error codes, rejection scenarios, performance characteristics, and examples.\n\n## Quick Reference: All Error Codes\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Category | Description |\n|------------|-------------|----------|-------------|\n| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Authentication | Authorization header or X-Amz-Credential query parameter is missing |\n| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | Authentication | The provided access key does not exist in credentials store |\n| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | 403 | Authentication | Calculated signature does not match the provided signature |\n| `InvalidAlgorithm` | 403 | Authentication | Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported |\n| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Authentication | Credential format is invalid (insufficient parts) |\n| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | Authentication | Authorization header is missing required fields |\n| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Authentication | Request timestamp is outside allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n| `MissingDateHeader` | 403 | Authentication | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n| `InvalidDateFormat` | 403 | Authentication | X-Amz-Date header format is invalid (not ISO 8601) |\n| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Authorization | ACL restrictions prevent access to requested bucket/key |\n| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | Request | HTTP method is not supported for the requested endpoint |\n| `InternalError` | 500 | Server | Server encountered an error during request processing |\n\n## Error Response Format\n\n## Error Response Format\n\nAll error responses follow the S3 XML error format with consistent headers:\n\n### HTTP Headers\n\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 403 Forbidden (for authentication errors)\n```\n\n### Response Body\n\n```xml\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>ErrorCode</Code>\n  <Message>Human-readable error description</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n## Authentication Error Codes\n\n### Invalid Credentials\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records. | Access key not found in credentials store |\n| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | 403 | The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. | Secret key is incorrect or signature is invalid |\n| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing authentication token. | Authorization header is completely missing |\n| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format. | Credential string has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n\n### Malformed Authorization Headers\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | The authorization header is malformed. | Required components missing (Credential, SignedHeaders, or Signature) |\n| `InvalidAlgorithm` | 403 | The authorization header algorithm is not supported. | Algorithm is not AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 |\n| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Access Denied. | Generic authentication failure |\n\n### Timing Issues\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired. | Request timestamp is outside the allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n| `MissingDateHeader` | 403 | Missing required header: x-amz-date. | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n| `InvalidDateFormat` | 403 | Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header. | X-Amz-Date header is not in ISO 8601 format (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ) |\n\n## Authorization and Access Control Errors\n\n### ACL-Based Access Denial\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Access Denied. | Credential exists but ACL restrictions prevent access to the requested bucket/key |\n\n**ACL Access Control Scenarios:**\n\nARMOR supports bucket and prefix-based access control lists (ACLs). When a credential has ACL restrictions configured, the following scenarios will result in `AccessDenied` errors:\n\n1. **Bucket Mismatch** - Credential's ACL bucket restriction doesn't match the requested bucket\n2. **Prefix Mismatch** - Credential's ACL prefix restriction doesn't match the requested key\n3. **No Matching ACL** - No ACL entry allows access to the requested bucket/key combination\n\n**Example:**\n\n```yaml\n# Credential configuration\ncredentials:\n  RESTRICTEDKEY:\n    access_key: RESTRICTEDKEY\n    secret_key: SECRET123...\n    acls:\n      - bucket: \"data-bucket\"\n        prefix: \"allowed/\"\n```\n\nWith this configuration:\n- ✓ `GET /data-bucket/allowed/file.txt` - Success\n- ✗ `GET /data-bucket/forbidden/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n- ✗ `GET /other-bucket/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n\n## Malformed Signature Scenarios\n\nARMOR validates signature format and provides specific error codes:\n\n| Scenario | Error Code | Performance |\n|----------|------------|-------------|\n| Non-hex signature characters | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n| Too short signature (< 32 bytes) | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n| Empty signature | `IncompleteSignature` | < 50ms |\n| Random characters in signature | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n\n## Query Parameter Authentication Errors\n\nARMOR supports authentication via presigned URLs using query parameters. These scenarios can result in authentication errors:\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing Authentication Token | X-Amz-Credential query parameter is missing |\n| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format | X-Amz-Credential has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist | Access key from credential parameter not found |\n| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | Authorization header is missing required fields | X-Amz-SignedHeaders or X-Amz-Signature query parameter is missing |\n| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired | Presigned URL has exceeded its expiration time (X-Amz-Expires) |\n\n**Presigned URL Format:**\n```\nhttps://bucket.s3.amazonaws.com/key?\n  X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&\n  X-Amz-Credential=ACCESSKEY/DATE/REGION/s3/aws4_request&\n  X-Amz-Date=YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ&\n  X-Amz-Expires=SECONDS&\n  X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&\n  X-Amz-Signature=CALCULATED_SIGNATURE\n```\n\n## Request Method Errors\n\nARMOR validates that the HTTP method is appropriate for the requested operation:\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | Method {METHOD} not allowed | HTTP method is not supported for the requested endpoint |\n\n**Examples:**\n- Unsupported POST operation on endpoints that only support GET\n- Using DELETE on read-only operations\n\n## Internal Server Errors\n\nThese errors indicate server-side problems during request processing:\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `InternalError` | 500 | Failed to {operation}: {error} | Server encountered an error during request processing |\n\n**Common InternalError Scenarios:**\n- Encryption key derivation failures\n- DEK generation failures\n- IV generation failures\n- Header encoding failures\n- Upload failures\n- Temporary file creation failures\n\n## Performance Guarantees\n\nAll authentication rejection scenarios respond within strict time limits:\n\n| Test Type | Target | Actual | Environment |\n|-----------|--------|--------|-------------|\n| Unit test rejections | < 100ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n| Integration test rejections | < 500ms | < 50ms | Real server |\n| Malformed signature rejections | < 50ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n\n### Performance Test Coverage\n\nThe test suite includes performance verification for all rejection scenarios:\n\n1. **TestInvalidCredentialRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 100ms response time\n2. **TestMalformedSignatureRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 50ms response time for malformed signatures\n\n## Error Message Quality\n\nAll error responses include:\n\n1. **Specific Error Code** - Identifies the exact problem (e.g., `InvalidAccessKeyId`, `SignatureDoesNotMatch`)\n2. **Meaningful Message** - Human-readable description of the problem\n3. **XML Format** - S3-compatible XML structure with proper escaping\n\n### Message Validation\n\nTest suite verifies that:\n- Error messages are never empty\n- Messages are at least 10 characters long\n- Messages contain relevant keywords (authentication, signature, credential, algorithm, header, aws4)\n- XML is properly escaped to prevent injection\n\n## Response Consistency\n\nAll error responses maintain consistency:\n\n### Headers\n- Always return `Content-Type: application/xml`\n- Always return appropriate HTTP status code (403 for auth errors)\n- Response body is never empty\n\n### Structure\n- XML declaration with encoding: `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`\n- Root element: `<Error>`\n- Two child elements: `<Code>` and `<Message>`\n- Proper XML escaping for special characters\n\n## Test Coverage Summary\n\nThe ARMOR test suite includes comprehensive coverage for rejection scenarios:\n\n### Unit Tests (`invalid_credential_test.go`)\n- 12 test scenarios covering:\n  - Invalid AWS credentials\n  - Malformed signatures  \n  - Missing authentication headers\n  - Malformed authorization headers\n  - Insufficient credential parts\n  - Missing required components (SignedHeaders, Signature, date)\n  - Expired requests\n  - Performance validation\n\n### Unit Tests (`malformed_signature_test.go`)\n- 20+ test scenarios covering:\n  - Garbage signature strings (non-hex, too short, empty, random chars)\n  - Invalid signature formats (missing algorithm, wrong algorithm, missing components)\n  - Partial signatures (missing components)\n  - Error message quality\n  - Performance validation\n\n### Unit Tests (`error_response_verification_test.go`)\n- Comprehensive verification of all acceptance criteria:\n  - Meaningful error messages for all rejection scenarios\n  - Error messages specify the rejection reason\n  - Response time under 100ms for all rejections\n  - Consistent response headers across rejection types\n  - Performance statistics and thresholds\n  - Documentation generation for error response format\n\n### Integration Tests (`invalid_credential_integration_test.go`)\n- Real server tests with actual HTTP client\n- Performance validation under realistic conditions\n- End-to-end verification of error responses\n\n### Headers Consistency (`error_response_test.go`)\n- Verifies consistent headers across all rejection types\n- Validates Content-Type header\n- Ensures proper XML structure\n\n### Authorization Tests (`auth_headers_doc_test.go`)\n- Documents authentication rejection response headers\n- Generates comprehensive header documentation\n- Verifies error codes and messages for all auth scenarios\n\n## Examples\n\n### Example 1: Invalid Access Key\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\nHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\nAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=INVALIDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=abc123...\nX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>InvalidAccessKeyId</Code>\n  <Message>The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records.</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 2: Missing Authentication\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\nHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n  <Message>Missing authentication token.</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 3: Expired Request\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\nHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\nAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=!@#$%^&*()\nX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>SignatureDoesNotMatch</Code>\n  <Message>The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided.</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 4: ACL Access Denied\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /protected-bucket/admin/config.yaml HTTP/1.1\nHost: protected-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\nAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=RESTRICTEDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=valid123...\nX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n```\n\n**Configuration:**\n```yaml\ncredentials:\n  RESTRICTEDKEY:\n    access_key: RESTRICTEDKEY\n    secret_key: SECRET123...\n    acls:\n      - bucket: \"protected-bucket\"\n        prefix: \"public/\"\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>AccessDenied</Code>\n  <Message>Access Denied</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 5: Expired Presigned URL\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /data-bucket/file.csv?\n  X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&\n  X-Amz-Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250701/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request&\n  X-Amz-Date=20250701T120000Z&\n  X-Amz-Expires=3600&\n  X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&\n  X-Amz-Signature=abc123...\nHTTP/1.1\nHost: data-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>RequestExpired</Code>\n  <Message>Request has expired</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 6: Invalid Date Format\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\nHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\nAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=valid123...\nX-Amz-Date: July-14-2025\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>InvalidDateFormat</Code>\n  <Message>Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n## Implementation\n\nError responses are generated by two implementations:\n\n1. **Server Handler** (`internal/server/server.go:796-805`)\n   ```go\n   func (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n       // XML generation with proper escaping\n   }\n   ```\n\n2. **Handlers Package** (`internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:2695-2704`)\n   ```go\n   func (h *Handlers) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n       // XML generation with proper escaping\n   }\n   ```\n\nBoth implementations ensure:\n- Consistent Content-Type header\n- Proper XML escaping to prevent injection\n- S3-compatible format\n\n## Testing\n\nTo run the error response test suite:\n\n```bash\n# Run all rejection tests\ngo test -v -run \"TestInvalidCredentialRejection|TestMalformedSignatureRejection\" ./internal/server/\n\n# Run headers consistency test\ngo test -v -run TestErrorResponseHeadersConsistency ./internal/server/\n\n# Run integration tests (requires INTEGRATION_TEST=1)\nINTEGRATION_TEST=1 go test -v -run TestInvalidCredentialsIntegration ./internal/server/\n```\n\n## Maintenance\n\nWhen adding new error scenarios:\n\n1. **Add test coverage** - Create tests in appropriate test file\n2. **Verify error code** - Use existing S3 error code when possible\n3. **Check performance** - Ensure response time < 100ms\n4. **Validate headers** - Confirm Content-Type and XML structure\n5. **Update this doc** - Document new error code and scenario\n\n## References\n\n- [S3 Error When a credential has ACL restrictions configured, the following scenarios will result in `AccessDenied` errors:\n+\n+1. **Bucket Mismatch** - Credential's ACL bucket restriction doesn't match the requested bucket\n+2. **Prefix Mismatch** - Credential's ACL prefix restriction doesn't match the requested key\n+3. **No Matching ACL** - No ACL entry allows access to the requested bucket/key combination\n+\n+**Example:**\n+\n+```yaml\n+# Credential configuration\n+credentials:\n+  RESTRICTEDKEY:\n+    access_key: RESTRICTEDKEY\n+    secret_key: SECRET123...\n+    acls:\n+      - bucket: \"data-bucket\"\n+        prefix: \"allowed/\"\n+```\n+\n+With this configuration:\n+- ✓ `GET /data-bucket/allowed/file.txt` - Success\n+- ✗ `GET /data-bucket/forbidden/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n+- ✗ `GET /other-bucket/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n \n ## Malformed Signature Scenarios\n \n@@ -62,6 +116,57 @@ ARMOR validates signature format and provides specific error codes:\n | Empty signature | `IncompleteSignature` | < 50ms |\n | Random characters in signature | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n \n+## Query Parameter Authentication Errors\n+\n+ARMOR supports authentication via presigned URLs using query parameters. When a credential has ACL restrictions configured, the following scenarios will result in `AccessDenied` errors:\n+\n+1. **Bucket Mismatch** - Credential's ACL bucket restriction doesn't match the requested bucket\n+2. **Prefix Mismatch** - Credential's ACL prefix restriction doesn't match the requested key\n+3. **No Matching ACL** - No ACL entry allows access to the requested bucket/key combination\n+\n+**Example:**\n+\n+```yaml\n+# Credential configuration\n+credentials:\n+  RESTRICTEDKEY:\n+    access_key: RESTRICTEDKEY\n+    secret_key: SECRET123...\n+    acls:\n+      - bucket: \"data-bucket\"\n+        prefix: \"allowed/\"\n+```\n+\n+With this configuration:\n+- ✓ `GET /data-bucket/allowed/file.txt` - Success\n+- ✗ `GET /data-bucket/forbidden/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n+- ✗ `GET /other-bucket/file.txt` - AccessDenied\n \n ## Malformed Signature Scenarios\n \n@@ -62,6 +116,57 @@ ARMOR validates signature format and provides specific error codes:\n | Empty signature | `IncompleteSignature` | < 50ms |\n | Random characters in signature | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n \n+## Query Parameter Authentication Errors\n+\n+ARMOR supports authentication via presigned URLs using query parameters. These scenarios can result in authentication errors:\n+\n+| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n+|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n+| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing Authentication Token | X-Amz-Credential query parameter is missing |\n+| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format | X-Amz-Credential has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n+| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist | Access key from credential parameter not found |\n+| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | Authorization header is missing required fields | X-Amz-SignedHeaders or X-Amz-Signature query parameter is missing |\n+| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired | Presigned URL has exceeded its expiration time (X-Amz-Expires) |\n+\n+**Presigned URL Format:**\n+```\n+https://bucket.s3.amazonaws.com/key?\n+  X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&\n+  X-Amz-Credential=ACCESSKEY/DATE/REGION/s3/aws4_request&\n+  X-Amz-Date=YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ&\n+  X-Amz-Expires=SECONDS&\n+  X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&\n+  X-Amz-Signature=CALCULATED_SIGNATURE\n+```","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}
